title: Kessler, Ronald C. | The Online Books Page
excerpts:
Section Title: [The Online Books Page](https://onlinebooks.library.upenn.edu/) > Ronald C. Kessler
Content:
|**Ronald C. Kessler** (born April 26, 1947) is an American professor at Harvard Medical School. His research focuses on the precision treatment of mental illness to determine the appropriate intervention for specific patients. He ranks among the most highly cited researchers in the world, with an h-index of 349 as of June 2025. He developed the Kessler psychological distress scale, which is designed to measure non-specific psychological distress. *(From [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ronald_C._Kessler) )*
**More about Ronald C. Kessler:**
[Resources in your library](https://onlinebooks.library.upenn.edu/webbin/ftl?su=Kessler%2c%20Ronald%20C%2e)

title: Ronald Kessler, New York Times Bestselling Author
excerpts: Kessler's *Washington Post* [stories](https://www.washingtonpost.com/archive/politics/1984/03/12/sponsors-claim-race-is-stumbling-block/7b342ef5-d066-4b37-b7fa-a4598b52ec8c/) exposing the fact that Lena Ferguson had been denied membership in the Daughters of the American Revolution (DAR) because she is black led to her acceptance by the DAR, her appointment as national vice chair of the D.C. Community Scholarship, and widespread changes in the organization's policies to increase membership by blacks. Kessler's quotes are on [BrainyQuote](https://www.brainyquote.com/authors/ronald-kessler-quotes) . For more Ronald Kessler articles go here .

[Depression: more treatment but no drop in prevalence](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/331643072_Depression_more_treatment_but_no_drop_in_prevalence)
Article
Full-text available
Mar 2019
[Johan Ormel](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Johan-Ormel)
[Ronald C. Kessler](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Ronald-Kessler)
[Robert Schoevers](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Robert-Schoevers)
Purpose of review: Since the 70s, treatment of depression, especially pharmacologically, has expanded enormously. However, epidemiological studies show that 12-month population prevalence rates have not dropped. This observation raises multiple questions. How good are treatments of depression actually? Do they improve long-term outcomes? Have the...
